program_name: MSS CRC IO Supporting Evidence Corpus

trials.primary_result: PFS HR 0.60; OS HR 0.74. Definitive positive for MSI-H.
trials.role_in_corpus: POSITIVE CONTROL — confirms IO works in MSI-H CRC. Defines the contrast with MSS CRC.
trials.transfer_lesson: MSI-H CRC responds to IO (PFS HR 0.60). MSS CRC does not respond to IO in unselected populations. The difference is neoantigen load (high in MSI-H, low in MSS) and TME phenotype (inflamed in MSI-H, excluded/desert in MSS).

trials.primary_result: MSS cohort ORR 0% (n=74). MSI-H ORR 31% mono, 55% combo.
trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — confirms IO does NOT work in unselected MSS CRC. ORR 0% in 74 MSS patients.
trials.transfer_lesson: Unselected MSS CRC: ORR 0%. The responder subgroup (NLM + pTMB ≥28 + inflamed TME) is too small to detect in unselected populations.

trials.primary_result: ORR 7% (5/70). All 5 responders had NO liver metastases. Non-liver-met ORR 22% (5/23).
trials.role_in_corpus: LIVER METASTASIS EVIDENCE — confirms liver metastasis as the critical IO stratification variable
trials.transfer_lesson: 0/5 liver-met responders. Non-liver-met ORR 22%. Liver metastasis is a contraindication for IO in MSS CRC.

trials.primary_result: CRC cohort (n=24 MSS): ORR 33%, median PFS 6.3 months. All 8 responders had NO liver metastases.
trials.role_in_corpus: LIVER METASTASIS EVIDENCE — Japan cohort confirms NLM enrichment
trials.transfer_lesson: All 8 responders had NO liver metastases. ORR 33% in NLM-enriched cohort vs 7% in unselected US cohort.

trials.primary_result: ORR 27.6% overall; NLM cohort ORR ~40%; liver-met ORR ~5%. Median OS 20 months in NLM cohort.
trials.role_in_corpus: HIGHEST REPORTED IO ORR IN MSS CRC — NLM cohort ORR ~40%
trials.transfer_lesson: NLM cohort ORR ~40% is the highest reported IO ORR in MSS CRC. This defines the ceiling for IO benefit in the NLM subpopulation.

trials.primary_result: ORR 17% (95% CI 10–26%) in no-liver-met MSS mCRC (n=101)
trials.role_in_corpus: NLM ENRICHMENT EVIDENCE — confirms NLM selection strategy
trials.transfer_lesson: ORR 17% in NLM-selected MSS mCRC. Botensilimab's Fc-enhanced CTLA-4 blockade (increased ADCC/ADCP) may be more effective than standard CTLA-4 blockade in MSS CRC.

trials.role_in_corpus: ONGOING RANDOMIZED PHASE III — only ongoing randomized Phase III in refractory MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: STELLAR-303 is the only ongoing randomized Phase III in refractory MSS CRC. Results will define the new standard of care for 3L+ MSS mCRC.

trials.drug: mKRAS vaccine (ELI-002 2P) — lipid-conjugated KRAS G12D/G12V peptides
trials.primary_result: ctDNA clearance in 3/5 CRC patients. mKRAS-specific T-cell responses confirmed.
trials.role_in_corpus: PHARMACODYNAMIC DISSOCIATION EVIDENCE — T-cell responses confirmed but no objective responses
trials.transfer_lesson: mKRAS vaccine activates T cells and clears ctDNA but does not produce objective responses. Pharmacodynamic dissociation in MSS CRC.

trials.primary_result: T-cell responses to ≥1 antigen in 100% of patients. No objective responses. Stable disease in some patients.
trials.transfer_lesson: 100% T-cell response rate, 0% ORR. The most extreme pharmacodynamic dissociation example in the corpus.

trials.primary_result: PFS HR 1.04 (95% CI 0.77–1.40) — no benefit. OS HR 0.91 — no benefit. All biomarker subgroups negative.
trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — IO addition to 1L mFOLFOX6+bev fails in unselected population
trials.transfer_lesson: Confirms QUILT-2.004 finding: IO addition to mFOLFOX6+bev in unselected mCRC does not improve outcomes.

trials.primary_result: ORR 27.6% (8/29 evaluable). Median OS 9.1 months. Post-hoc: patients without liver metastases had better outcomes.
trials.role_in_corpus: LIVER METASTASIS EVIDENCE — post-hoc NLM benefit
trials.transfer_lesson: ORR 27.6% in small cohort — likely enriched for NLM patients. Post-hoc NLM benefit consistent with CO.26 and REGONIVO.

trials.primary_result: No T-cell increase; no objective responses.
trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — DNMT inhibition does not enhance GVAX in MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: Epigenetic priming (DNMT inhibition) does not overcome pharmacodynamic dissociation in MSS CRC.

trials.drug: PANVAC (CEA/MUC1 poxvirus vaccine) + Dendritic cell vaccine
trials.primary_result: No OS benefit; T-cell responses >70% of patients.
trials.transfer_lesson: T-cell responses >70%, no OS benefit. Pharmacodynamic dissociation confirmed across multiple vaccine platforms.

trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — oncolytic virus + IO fails in unselected MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: Oncolytic virus does not overcome immune exclusion in MSS CRC without patient selection.

trials.role_in_corpus: NEGATIVE CONTROL — TGF-β + PD-L1 dual blockade fails in unselected MSS CRC
trials.transfer_lesson: Even TGF-β + PD-L1 dual blockade fails in unselected MSS CRC. Patient selection (immune-excluded vs immune-desert) is required to identify patients who can benefit from stromal barrier disruption.

what_crispro_proved: RIN NLM subgroup: ORR ~40%, mOS 20 months — highest reported IO ORR in MSS CRC
BOT/BAL Phase 1: ORR 17% (95% CI 10–26%) in no-liver-met MSS mCRC (n=101)
KEYNOTE-177 (MSI-H): PFS HR=0.60 — positive control confirming IO works in MSI-H
CheckMate 142 MSS cohort: ORR 0% (n=74) — confirms immune exclusion in unselected MSS CRC
STELLAR-303 (zanzalintinib + atezolizumab): only ongoing randomized Phase III in refractory MSS CRC
Pharmacodynamic dissociation confirmed across 4 vaccine platforms (GVAX, PolyPEPI1018, PANVAC, AMPLIFY-201)
